item.title: About 3D Print Metal
item.description:
Precision Metal Parts. Industrial-Grade Additive Manufacturing.

3D Print Metal is a specialized resource covering metal additive manufacturing for engineers, materials scientists, and procurement officers. We cover DMLS, SLM, and EBM process parameters, alloy selection, post-processing requirements, and supplier qualification with the technical depth that generalist publications miss.

Ben Stagl (SAIC professor, 20-year fabrication veteran) and Max Davis (MAXCNC) bring hands-on manufacturing expertise to the highest-stakes vertical in additive manufacturing. Metal DMLS parts at $2,000–$20,000 per build have no margin for ambiguity.

item.title: Advertise on 3D Print Metal
item.description:
Advertise on 3D Print Metal

Our audience is the highest-value segment in additive manufacturing: engineers and procurement officers sourcing DMLS and SLM metal parts for aerospace structures, medical implants, and critical industrial components. These are $10K–$500K purchase decisions.

Audience Profile

  • Job titles: Aerospace structures engineer, medical device engineer, procurement officer, materials scientist
  • Industry: Aerospace (OEM and tier-1), medical devices, motorsport, defence, energy
  • Decision value: Metal AM parts typically $2,000–$50,000 per production run

Advertising Packages

Package Rate Placement
Banner Ad (728×90) $500/month Homepage leaderboard
Sponsored Content $1,200/month Technical article feature
Network Package $3,500/month All PrintWire metal sites

Ideal partners: DMLS service bureaus, metal powder suppliers, HIP/heat treatment facilities, DfAM and simulation software.

item.title: Metal 3D Printing Services
item.description:
DMLS & SLM Printing

Our Direct Metal Laser Sintering and Selective Laser Melting systems use high-powered lasers to fuse metal powder layer by layer, producing fully dense parts with mechanical properties comparable to wrought material. Industrial platforms with build volumes up to 400 × 400 × 400 mm process titanium, stainless steel, Inconel, aluminum, cobalt chrome, and tool steels. Parts achieve densities exceeding 99.5% with surface finishes as fine as Ra 6–10 µm as-built.
